mnemonics.title: KICKSTART Components
mnemonics.content: KICKSTART: Keep Installation Configured - Know System Tasks Easily Without Effort - Configuration tool using file named ks.cfg. Remember: K-S-C-F-G (Keyboard, System, Configuration, Files, Gateway)

mnemonics.title: PXE Boot Sequence
mnemonics.content: PXE Steps: D - H - C - T - I (DHCP request → Host IP → Connect TFTP → Download Boot → Install OS)

mnemonics.title: Automated Installation Benefits
mnemonics.content: SCRAP: Standardized Configurations, Reduced Errors, Cost-effective, Rapid deployment, Automated provisioning

acronyms.usage: Network boot protocol enabling OS installation without local media
Microsoft technology for network-based Windows OS deployment
Debian-based automated installation tool for large-scale deployments
Lightweight protocol used to transfer boot files in PXE environment
Automatically assigns IP addresses and provides boot information to PXE clients
Red Hat/CentOS automated installation configuration file (ks.cfg)
Industry-standard tool for initializing cloud computing instances

visualTips.visualization: Draw a flow: Client → DHCP Request → DHCP Response (IP+TFTP info) → TFTP Download (boot image) → Installation Server → OS Install. Visualize as a pipeline: DHCP → TFTP → INSTALLER → OS
Think of it as a 3-layer cake: Bottom = Basic config (keyboard, language, timezone), Middle = Storage (partitioning), Top = Software & Scripts (packages, %post)
Show cycle: Build Master System → Configure → Test → Capture Image → Store → Deploy to Multiple Targets → Monitor → Update Master → Repeat

analogies.analogy: Like a pre-recorded macro in Excel - you record all the steps once (configuration), then replay it infinitely (deployment) without manual input each time.
Like photocopying a filled-out form - instead of filling each form manually, you make one perfect copy and replicate it exactly.
Like a computer booting from network just like humans learning from remote teachers - the knowledge (boot files) comes through network (teacher) rather than local books (hard drive).
Like an online form with autofill enabled - all answers are pre-filled, user just clicks submit (installation runs automatically).
